page: Microsoft Azure E16s_v4 is an ESv4-family virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz and includes 48 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. It does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark evaluations place the E16s_v4 primarily in the average performance tier. The instance achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 1410.0 and a multi-core score of 8567.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 690.78 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read and write speeds reached 96.64 GB/sec and 55.64 GB/sec, respectively. In database-centric workloads, the server processed 12.92 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 5319.93 on PassMark database operations.

While multi-threaded operations perform consistently in the middle tier, certain single-threaded tasks exhibit performance limitations. Specifically, single-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak at 8.63 GB/sec. Additionally, while prompt processing for a small 135M LLM model is average at 750.7 tokens/sec, prompt processing for a medium 7B model drops to a weak tier at 24.19 tokens/sec. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and Premium Storage compatibility, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, enterprise applications, and caching layers, though it is less optimal for heavy single-threaded decompression or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v4 is an ESv4-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance features 48 MB of L3 cache and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 8567.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 12.92 million operations per second. However, single-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM prompt processing show relative weakness. This resource density makes the instance suitable for memory-optimized workloads such as relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without high local storage demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v4 is an ESv4-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v4 is an Intel-based virtual machine with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, designed for memory-optimized database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated Intel vCPUs and substantial memory capacity for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 48 MB L3 cache
128.0 GB of system memory supporting memory-intensive applications
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or integrated GPUs
Average multi-core performance and 12.92 million Redis SET operations per second
Weak single-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM prompt processing
Best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
